rationale: Neither candidate hits the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about whether hasattr(reduce_scatter) checks are sufficient verification), so recall is low for both. X supplies more grounded, file:line-specific, actionable findings (including a genuine test-coverage gap on the DP-metadata early-return branches), while Y's review is a well-verified but higher-level report with only one concrete actionable comment and an APPROVE table. Y's technical checks (DPMetadata dataclass validity, destroy_model_parallel cleanup) are slightly more insightful and precise, but X's greater volume of concrete, correctly-grounded line items edges it ahead on actionability.
